title: achillea borealis subsp. californica
intro: Achillea borealis subsp. californica, also known as California yarrow, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant contains compounds like thujone and volatile oils that offer health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. California yarrow can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for various herbal preparations.
